methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Hadrian, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company founded by Chris Power, is valued at $1.6B post-money following $560M in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of nine comparables—eight private robotics firms at Series B through Series D stages and one public company—reflecting the nascent public market presence in this sector.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to Hadrian's $1.6B last post-money valuation and applying a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier for Series C progression, implies a $2.7B valuation. Peer-median analysis across eight same-sector, same/adjacent-stage competitors yields a $1.3B implied value, with peer valuations ranging from $300M to $10.0B. The five largest private comparables include Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B).

The triangulated range spans $1.1B (low) to $3.1B (high), with a median of $2.0B. This median sits materially above Hadrian's current post-money, reflecting both stage-progression expectations and the wide dispersion within the peer set. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the $10.0B outlier (1X) and the limited public market comparability, which constrains confidence in market-based multiples for this stage and sector.
